standard: Agentic Governance & Security Controls by COHUMAIN Labs

controls.name: Autonomous System Oversight
controls.objective: Continuously observe autonomous agent behaviour against a defined baseline and enable timely human intervention.
key_requirements: Behavioural baseline documented per agent (expected action types, frequency, value/scope limits) and signed off before go-live.
≥99% of autonomous actions emit a structured trace (actor, action, target, rationale) within 1 second of execution.
≥5 deviation thresholds defined (e.g., out-of-pattern tool calls, action-rate spikes) each wired to an alert and a named responder.
Monthly oversight review; full re-test after any model, prompt, or tool change.
evidence: Signed, versioned behavioural-baseline specification.
Action-trace sample with timestamps demonstrating <1s emission.
Threshold/alert configuration export + 3 most-recent triggered alerts with dispositions.
Monthly oversight-review minutes with reviewer sign-off.
controls.risk_example: A procurement agent told to 'minimise stock-outs' begins auto-raising purchase orders nightly; the 6× volume shift goes unnoticed for two weeks because uptime monitoring only watched availability, not behaviour.
controls.review_cadence: Monthly review and testing, with additional testing after major updates, retraining, or changes to autonomous behavior.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
5.2 Loss of human agency and autonomy
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
7.6 Multi-agent risks
mappings.mit_risk_closed: An autonomous agent pursues its objective in ways that diverge from human intent (reward-hacking, goal mis-generalisation) and humans lose practical control as decisions are delegated to the system.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.TA0015 Command and Control (tactic, new in v5.1.0)
AML.T…
mitre_attack: Agentic Orchestration* Autonomous killchain orchestration (no ATT&CK ID yet)
mappings.attack_defense_note: Detects the autonomous killchain orchestration the Navigator flags as the top unscored risk.

controls.name: Emergency Stop & Containment (Kill Switch)
controls.objective: Provide a single, fast mechanism to halt, contain and isolate an AI system on a safety or security trigger.
key_requirements: Single authorized mechanism that on activation halts execution, revokes the agent's credentials, and isolates affected components — covering BOTH the safety-triggered halt and the security-triggered containment scenario.
Halt achieved within a defined SLA (target ≤5s) at infrastructure AND application layers.
Activation restricted to named roles — AI Safety Lead (safety halt) and Cybersecurity (security containment); 100% of activations logged with actor, reason, scope.
Monthly test for agentic/high-risk systems; documented recovery procedure; explicitly linked to the incident-response workflow.
evidence: Emergency-stop/containment procedure naming both activation modes and authorized roles.
Monthly halt-test records with measured halt latency vs SLA.
Activation/audit log sample + evidence of credential revocation and component isolation.
Recovery-drill record and linked IR runbook reference.
controls.risk_example: An email-integrated agent is compromised by indirect prompt injection and starts exfiltrating documents. Operators need ONE action that stops it, revokes its token, and isolates the connector — not a safety halt and a separate security process racing each other.
controls.review_cadence: Monthly test (agentic/high-risk)

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
7.2 AI possessing dangerous capabilities
5.2 Loss of human agency and autonomy
4.2 Cyberattacks, weapon development or use, and mass harm
mappings.mit_risk_closed: A misaligned agent runs away at machine speed (safety), OR a hijacked agent is weaponised and chains malicious actions across systems (security). In both cases there is no single, fast way to stop, contain and isolate it.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.TA0015 Command and Control (tactic, new in v5.1.0)
AML.T…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Containment severs C2 and halts exfiltration / remote control by a weaponised agent.

controls.name: Content Safety & Misuse Prevention
controls.objective: Prevent generation of harmful, unsafe or policy-violating content and resist misuse and jailbreaks.
key_requirements: Inline input/output content filters active on 100% of generative responses (toxicity, harmful instructions, regulated-claim categories).
Documented red-team test set exercised pre-deployment and after each model/prompt change, with a defined pass threshold.
Safety-violation monitoring + escalation path; user-report mechanism for problematic outputs.
evidence: Filter policy/config + proof of coverage across all endpoints.
Red-team test report: attempt categories, success rate, remediation.
Blocked/escalated-output log and user-report queue with dispositions.
controls.risk_example: A customer-facing assistant is jailbroken into producing prohibited or unsafe instructions (e.g., enabling fraud or harm) that no content control intercepts.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment factual accuracy validation.
Periodic output review based on risk tier and use frequency.
Monthly evaluation for high-risk or decision-support systems.
Additional review after model changes, prompt changes, retrieval-source changes, or detected factual errors.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
1.2 Exposure to toxic content
4.3 Fraud, scams, and targeted manipulation
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
1.1 Unfair discrimination and misrepresentation
3.1 False or misleading information
mappings.mit_risk_closed: A generative system produces harmful, toxic, or abusive content, or is jailbroken into enabling misuse (fraud, manipulation, harmful instructions).
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0054 LLM Jailbreak
AML.T0048 External Harms (Financial …
mappings.attack_defense_note: Prevents the model being misused to develop malware/obfuscation — the most common AI-assisted attack techniques observed.

controls.name: Output Factual Accuracy & Anti-Hallucination
controls.objective: Ensure factual grounding of generative outputs and detect and mitigate hallucination.
key_requirements: Groundedness/factuality benchmark defined per use case with a minimum acceptable score.
Source attribution/citation enabled for fact-bearing outputs; uncertainty signalled when confidence is low.
Periodic factuality evaluation against a trusted reference set; monthly for decision-support uses.
evidence: Benchmark definition + most-recent scored results vs threshold.
Sample outputs showing citations / uncertainty flags.
Evaluation log + remediation actions on detected drift.
controls.risk_example: A knowledge assistant fabricates a confident citation to a non-existent source; a user relays it as fact, creating misinformation and liability exposure.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment factual accuracy validation.
Periodic output review based on risk tier and use frequency.
Monthly evaluation for high-risk or decision-support systems.
Additional review after model changes, prompt changes, retrieval-source changes, or detected factual errors.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
3.1 False or misleading information
5.1 Overreliance and unsafe use
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
3.2 Pollution of information ecosystem / loss of consensus reality
mappings.mit_risk_closed: A GenAI system fabricates fluent, confident but false output; users over-rely on it and make harmful decisions on false beliefs.
mappings.mitre_atlas: AML.T0048 External Harms (Financial / Reputational / Societa…
mappings.attack_defense_note: N/A — factual-accuracy / safety control, not an adversarial-security technique.

controls.name: AI Output Monitoring & Safeguards
controls.objective: Monitor AI outputs at runtime for bias, harm, hallucination and drift, gating regulated decisions to humans.
key_requirements: Runtime monitoring of output for bias, hallucination, harmful content and quality drift, with defined thresholds.
Human-in-the-loop gate mandatory before any AI output enters a regulated decision or process.
High-risk: continuous monitoring + monthly review; documented escalation on threshold breach.
evidence: Monitoring dashboard + threshold configuration.
HITL gate evidence (approval records on regulated outputs).
Monthly review report + escalation tickets.
controls.risk_example: A triage/prioritisation model gradually drifts and begins down-ranking critical cases; with no output monitoring the degradation surfaces only at the next audit.
controls.review_cadence:
High Risk: Continuous monitoring with monthly review.
Low Risk: Monitoring aligned to use frequency and business impact.
Additional review should occur when harmful, biased, misleading, or degraded outputs are detected.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
3.1 False or misleading information
1.1 Unfair discrimination and misrepresentation
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
1.2 Exposure to toxic content
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Output quality silently degrades — bias, hallucination, harmful content, drift — and reaches regulated decisions without human review.
mappings.mitre_atlas: AML.T0048 External Harms (Financial / Reputational / Societa…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Detects harmful generated artefacts and anomalous output/exfiltration volumes.

controls.name: Autonomy Boundary Control
controls.objective: Constrain each agent to an explicit, technically-enforced action space.
key_requirements: Declarative allow/deny action policy per agent: permitted actions, restricted actions, value/scope caps, approval-required actions.
Hard technical enforcement at the gateway (deny-by-default); boundaries cannot be overridden by prompt content.
Boundary-violation detection with real-time block + alert; 100% of attempts logged.
Quarterly enforcement test: attempt each restricted action and confirm it is blocked.
evidence: Versioned action-policy file per agent.
Gateway configuration showing deny-by-default and enforced caps.
Violation log + quarterly enforcement-test results (attempted vs blocked).
controls.risk_example: A data agent scoped to READ a production database is asked by a crafted prompt to 'correct' a value; with no hard write-boundary it issues an UPDATE and corrupts a system-of-record.
controls.review_cadence:
Before deployment for systems with autonomous or tool-use capability.
Quarterly review for systems with ongoing autonomous behavior.
Additional review after material changes to tools, workflows, permissions, integrations, or system capabilities.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
5.2 Loss of human agency and autonomy
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.2 AI possessing dangerous capabilities
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
mappings.mit_risk_closed: An agent takes actions outside its intended scope — invoking tools, writing to systems, or spending — because no hard limit constrains its action space (excessive agency).
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0053 Compromise LLM Plugins / Tools
AML.T0048 External …
mappings.attack_defense_note: Bounds the action space so a hijacked agent cannot execute unauthorised commands or remote control.

controls.name: Human Oversight & Intervention
controls.objective: Guarantee accessible, timely human review, intervention and override of AI decisions and actions.
key_requirements: Accessible review/intervene/override/escalate mechanism with defined operator responsibilities and intervention triggers.
For agents: oversight is pre-emptive (approval gate before a high-impact action), not only after-the-fact review.
evidence: Oversight design + operator runbook with triggers.
Intervention/override log; approval-gate records for agents.
controls.risk_example: An agent executes an irreversible action (submits a filing, sends funds, deletes records) before any human can review — oversight existed only as after-the-fact reporting.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment review of oversight design.
Periodic review based on risk tier and use frequency.
Testing before high-risk systems go live.
Additional review after incidents, user feedback, workflow changes, autonomy changes, or changes to decision impact.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
5.2 Loss of human agency and autonomy
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
5.1 Overreliance and unsafe use
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Humans cannot intervene in AI-driven decisions, losing agency; for agents, after-the-fact review is too slow to prevent harm.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.TA0015 Command and Control (tactic, new in v5.1.0)
AML.T…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Human gate before consequential/irreversible actions limits autonomous exfiltration and C2-driven actions.

controls.name: Decision Interpretability
controls.objective: Provide audience-appropriate explanations for AI outputs affecting people's rights, safety or health.
key_requirements: Audience-appropriate explanation for outputs affecting health, safety or rights (EU AI Act Art.13).
Explanation available at the point of decision to the affected person/operator.
evidence: Interpretability design doc + example explanations per audience.
Evidence the explanation is surfaced in the UI / decision record.
controls.risk_example: An individual affected by an AI safety decision is given no understandable reason, breaching transparency obligations. (Transparency control — ATLAS not directly applicable.)
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment interpretability review for applicable systems.
Periodic review where AI recommendations influence decisions or operations.
Additional review after model, prompt, data, decision logic, or use-case changes.
Enhanced review for higher-impact or regulated decision contexts.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary: 7.4 Lack of transparency or interpretability
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
5.1 Overreliance and unsafe use
1.1 Unfair discrimination and misrepresentation
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Users affected by AI decisions (health, safety, rights) receive no understandable explanation, eroding trust and recourse.
mappings.mitre_atlas: N/A — not adversarial-security (transparency/sustainability …
mappings.attack_defense_note: N/A — transparency control; supports forensic attribution across techniques.

controls.name: Model Explainability
controls.objective: Make model decision logic explainable for accountability, audit and error-correction.
key_requirements: Risk-appropriate explanation method documented for any AI affecting users or regulated decisions.
For GenAI/agentic: reasoning trace / decision rationale / citations serve as the explanation artefact.
evidence: Explanation-method description + sample explanations.
For agents: decision/action trace samples.
controls.risk_example: A decision model denies a request and the team cannot explain why, so it can neither defend the decision to an auditor nor correct the error.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment explainability validation.
Periodic explanation quality review.
Stakeholder comprehension review for higher-risk systems.
Annual compliance or governance review where applicable.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary: 7.4 Lack of transparency or interpretability
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
1.1 Unfair discrimination and misrepresentation
5.1 Overreliance and unsafe use
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Opaque model decisions cannot be understood, challenged, or audited, undermining accountability and error-correction.
mappings.mitre_atlas: N/A — not adversarial-security (transparency/sustainability …
mappings.attack_defense_note: N/A — transparency control; aids incident forensics.

controls.name: Output Labeling & Action Attribution
controls.objective: Label AI-generated content and attribute autonomous agent actions to the agent.
key_requirements: AI-generated content clearly labelled where human origin might be assumed; agent ACTIONS attributed to the agent (EU AI Act Art.50).
Labelling persists downstream (not stripped on export).
evidence: Labelling standard + sample labelled outputs/actions.
Downstream-persistence check.
controls.risk_example: An AI-drafted document is mistaken for human-authored; with no label, accountability and consent expectations are breached.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment validation of labeling controls.
Periodic review for externally facing, user-facing, or downstream-system outputs.
Additional review after changes to output channels, user groups, workflow routing, or transparency requirements.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
3.1 False or misleading information
5.1 Overreliance and unsafe use
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
3.2 Pollution of information ecosystem / loss of consensus reality
4.3 Fraud, scams, and targeted manipulation
mappings.mit_risk_closed: AI-generated content or agent actions are mistaken for human origin, enabling deception, overreliance, or impersonation.
mappings.mitre_atlas: AML.T0048 External Harms (Financial / Reputational / Societa…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Attribution/labelling counters AI-enabled impersonation and social engineering.

controls.name: Data Provenance & Input Integrity
controls.objective: Establish lineage and integrity of training and runtime inputs, including retrieval and tool data.
key_requirements: Lineage documented for training data AND runtime inputs (RAG sources, tool outputs, agent messages): source, transformation, intended use.
Source verification + ingestion-time validation; untrusted/external content flagged and isolated.
Audit trail for all data access and modification.
evidence: Data-lineage/provenance manifest (training + runtime).
Ingestion-validation logs + RAG source allow-list.
Access/modification audit trail.
controls.risk_example: A poisoned document is indexed into the RAG store; the agent now confidently repeats attacker-planted text as 'company policy'.
controls.review_cadence:
Before training or deployment for initial data validation.
At each material data source, ingestion, or transformation change.
Periodic review based on risk tier and data sensitivity.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
1.1 Unfair discrimination and misrepresentation
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
3.1 False or misleading information
2.1 Compromise of privacy (leaking / inferring sensitive info)
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Training data, RAG sources, tool outputs, or agent messages are poisoned or spoofed, corrupting model behaviour or injecting bias.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0020 Poison Training Data
AML.T0010 ML Supply Chain Com…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Provenance + input validation counter data-poisoning and supply-chain manipulation.

controls.name: AI Change Governance
controls.objective: Govern behaviour-altering AI changes (model, prompt, data, tool, config) with re-evaluation triggers.
key_requirements: AI-change taxonomy in force (model, prompt, data source, tool, config, fine-tune) — each a governed change type.
Material AI change triggers re-evaluation (model eval + threat model + security test) before release.
Change record links to the artefacts changed and the re-evaluation evidence.
evidence: AI-change register with type tags.
Re-evaluation evidence attached to material changes.
Approval/sign-off records.
controls.risk_example: Someone edits the system prompt to 'be more helpful'; the change bypasses the code-based CR process and quietly weakens a safety instruction.
controls.review_cadence:
Before deployment of any material AI system change.
Post-change monitoring after approved changes are released.
Periodic change review based on risk tier.
Additional review after unexpected behavior, drift, incidents, major vendor updates, or changes to intended use.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
6.5 Governance failure
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
mappings.mit_risk_closed: A model swap, prompt edit, new RAG source, or added tool silently changes system behaviour and risk — invisible to traditional change tickets — creating ungoverned drift.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0018 Manipulate / Backdoor ML Model
AML.T0010 ML Supply…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Governs model/tool/prompt changes to prevent malicious or unreviewed modification.

controls.name: Third-Party & Supply-Chain Oversight
controls.objective: Assess and contractually bind AI suppliers and the model supply chain.
key_requirements: AI-specific contract clauses: model/data provenance, training-data IP, transparency, change/deprecation notice, AI-incident notification, output indemnity.
AI risk assessment of supplier pre-contract; sub-processor / model chain disclosed.
Ongoing monitoring of supplier model changes and incident notifications.
evidence: Executed contract with the AI clause set.
Supplier AI risk assessment + sub-processor list.
Change/incident notification log from the supplier.
controls.risk_example: A foundation-model vendor silently deprecates the model version underpinning a validated production tool; outputs shift and the validation is void with no notice.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-contract / pre-procurement review where third-party AI is being selected. 

Pre-deployment / onboarding review before supplier-managed AI is used in a production solution. 

Annual review for High Risk third-party AI use cases, or more frequently based on criticality, incidents, regulatory exposure, or supplier dependency. 

Event-driven reassessment after material supplier changes, incidents, updated terms, model updates, data handling changes, hosting changes, integration changes, security posture changes, or regulatory-status changes.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
6.5 Governance failure
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
2.1 Compromise of privacy (leaking / inferring sensitive info)
3.1 False or misleading information
6.6 Environmental harm
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Foundation-model and agent-platform suppliers introduce opaque risks — undisclosed training data/IP, silent model updates, weak eval transparency, no AI-incident notification.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0010 ML Supply Chain Compromise
AML.T0058 Publish Poiso…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Third-party due-diligence counters supply-chain compromise and trusted-relationship abuse.

controls.name: Model Training Governance
controls.objective: Ensure secure, reproducible, bias-screened model training with documented data lineage.
key_requirements: Documented data lineage + approved, access-controlled training environment; reproducible pipeline; version control.
Datasets screened for bias/representativeness and protected (no unauthorized PII/IP).
evidence: Training-data lineage + bias-screening report.
Pipeline version/config + environment access records.
controls.risk_example: Training data quietly includes a competitor's licensed dataset; the resulting model carries IP contamination into production.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-training data quality validation.
Bias and integrity checks during development.
Periodic model performance review during development.
Regulatory or compliance review where applicable.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
1.1 Unfair discrimination and misrepresentation
2.1 Compromise of privacy (leaking / inferring sensitive info)
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
1.3 Unequal performance across groups
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Poorly sourced or unprotected training data introduces bias, unequal performance, or leakage of sensitive/IP data into the model.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0020 Poison Training Data
AML.T0018 Manipulate / Backdo…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Training-data integrity counters poisoning via the data supply chain.

controls.name: Model Evaluation
controls.objective: Validate model quality, bias, robustness and use-case suitability before deployment.
key_requirements: Risk-tiered evaluation criteria (quality, bias, robustness, use-case suitability) defined pre-deployment; results documented vs thresholds.
Domain/business validation sign-off for higher-risk uses.
evidence: Evaluation report with metrics vs acceptance thresholds.
Domain sign-off record.
controls.risk_example: A model validated only on overall accuracy performs far worse for a minority subgroup — undetected without subgroup evaluation.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment evaluation.
Monthly or periodic performance assessment where applicable.
Quarterly domain or business validation review for higher-risk systems.
Annual revalidation or reassessment where the model or use case materially changes.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
1.3 Unequal performance across groups
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
1.1 Unfair discrimination and misrepresentation
3.1 False or misleading information
mappings.mit_risk_closed: A model with inadequate predictive quality, bias, or unsuitability for the use case is deployed.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0043 Craft Adversarial Data
AML.T0031 Erode ML Model In…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Light — robustness evaluation reduces exploitability; not a direct ATT&CK technique.

controls.name: Model Continuous Improvement & Drift Management
controls.objective: Manage production drift with monitoring thresholds and retraining triggers.
key_requirements: Production performance/drift thresholds defined and monitored continuously.
Retraining/adjustment trigger and procedure on threshold breach.
evidence: Drift-monitoring dashboard + threshold configuration.
Retraining trigger log + post-retrain validation.
controls.risk_example: Input data distribution shifts after a label change; model accuracy decays for months because no drift threshold was set.
controls.review_cadence:
Real-time or periodic monitoring with automated alerts where applicable.
Weekly performance trend analysis where needed.
Monthly drift assessment.
Quarterly improvement planning.
Annual model lifecycle review.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary: 7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
1.3 Unequal performance across groups
3.1 False or misleading information
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Model performance drifts as input data evolves, degrading reliability in production.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0031 Erode ML Model Integrity
AML.T0020 Poison Training…
mappings.attack_defense_note: N/A — drift management; not an adversarial-security technique.

controls.name: AI Lifecycle & Registry Management
controls.objective: Version and govern all AI artefacts (model, prompts, tools, memory, agent configs) across the lifecycle.
key_requirements: All AI artefacts versioned and governed across the lifecycle: model AND prompts, tools, RAG sources, agent/memory configs.
Deployment records + post-deployment monitoring tied to each version.
evidence: Artefact registry with versions across all types.
Deployment + monitoring records per version.
controls.risk_example: Nobody can say which prompt + model + tool combination was live when an incident occurred, because only the model was versioned.
controls.review_cadence:
Lifecycle evidence review before deployment.
Ongoing monitoring after deployment for applicable models.
Periodic lifecycle review based on risk tier.
Revalidation after material model, data, prompt, configuration, or deployment changes.
Annual lifecycle review for high-risk or business-critical models.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
6.5 Governance failure
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
7.4 Lack of transparency or interpretability
mappings.mit_risk_closed: AI artefacts (model, and for AI: prompts, tools, memory, agent configs) are untracked across the lifecycle, causing ungoverned, unversioned, unmonitored behaviour.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0018 Manipulate / Backdoor ML Model
AML.T0010 ML Supply…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Versioned-artefact integrity counters tampering across the lifecycle.

controls.name: Environmental & Carbon Governance
controls.objective: Estimate and govern the environmental and carbon footprint of AI training, hosting and inference.
key_requirements: Carbon estimate across training, hosting and inference (including agentic multi-step loops) using a documented methodology (e.g., GHG Protocol).
Estimate produced to inform the architecture choice and recorded in the solution record.
evidence: Carbon calculation worksheet + methodology reference.
Architecture decision note citing the estimate.
controls.risk_example: An agentic workflow loops tool-calls 40× per task; un-estimated, it 10×'s inference cost and carbon versus the design assumption. (ATLAS maps only loosely via resource-exhaustion techniques — this is a sustainability control, not adversarial.)
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment carbon assessment.
Monthly production monitoring where applicable.
Quarterly carbon review for material compute usage.
Annual sustainability review for high-impact or large-scale AI systems.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary: 6.6 Environmental harm
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Training, hosting and (for agents) recurring multi-step inference loops consume energy and carbon at scale.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0034 Cost Harvesting
AML.T0029 Denial of ML Service
mappings.attack_defense_note: N/A — sustainability control.

controls.name: Multi-Agent Coordination Security
controls.objective: Secure inter-agent communication, hierarchy and emergent behaviour in multi-agent systems.
key_requirements: Interaction map of all agents: roles, who-calls-whom, decision hierarchy, escalation paths.
Documented inter-agent communication protocol with loop / deadlock / cascade safeguards.
Multi-agent simulation test before production and after any topology change; access control for non-the organisation agents.
evidence: Versioned agent interaction map + protocol specification.
Simulation-test report including failure-injection cases.
Access-control configuration for cross-organisation agent calls.
controls.risk_example: A 'reviewer' agent and a 'writer' agent enter a self-reinforcing loop, each accepting the other's output, mass-generating erroneous regulatory text overnight.
controls.review_cadence: Quarterly review and testing, or after material architecture, access, or agent interaction changes.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary: 7.6 Multi-agent risks
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Multiple agents interacting produce emergent failures — cascading errors, collusion, conflict, or new security vulnerabilities — that no single agent's design predicts.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.TA0015 Command and Control (tactic, new in v5.1.0)
AML.T…
mitre_attack: Agentic Orchestration* Autonomous killchain orchestration (no ATT&CK ID yet)
mappings.attack_defense_note: Governs inter-agent scaffolding — the exact mechanism (MCP-chained agents) behind the highest-risk real-world operation observed.

controls.name: Agent Identity & Least-Privilege Authorization
controls.objective: Give each agent a unique identity with just-in-time least-privilege authorization.
key_requirements: Each agent has a unique, non-shared machine identity provisioned before production.
Just-in-time, task-scoped permission grants; no standing broad permissions permitted.
Human-in-the-loop confirmation for high-impact actions; quarterly entitlement review of agent identities.
evidence: Agent identity registry (one identity per agent).
JIT grant/revoke logs + entitlement-review record.
Approval records for high-impact actions.
controls.risk_example: Ten agents share one service account with broad write access; one is compromised and the blast radius becomes every system that account can touch.
controls.review_cadence:
Before production deployment for all applicable agents.
Quarterly review of agent identities, permissions, and access scope.
Additional review after changes to tools, APIs, data sources, workflows, permissions, or system integrations.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
2.1 Compromise of privacy (leaking / inferring sensitive info)
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
4.3 Fraud, scams, and targeted manipulation
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Agents share identities or hold standing broad permissions; a single compromise or prompt-injection escalates into wide unauthorized access or data leakage.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0055 Unsecured Credentials
AML.T0051 LLM Prompt Injecti…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Unique agent identity + JIT least-privilege counter the credential-abuse and valid-account techniques most correlated with high-risk actors.

controls.name: Prompt Injection & Adversarial Input Defense
controls.objective: Detect and block direct and indirect prompt-injection and adversarial inputs.
key_requirements: LLM input/output firewall at every ingestion boundary; configuration reviewed quarterly.
Injection-detection rules in SIEM/SOC with alert thresholds and named owners; covers direct AND indirect (RAG/tool) vectors.
Untrusted content isolated from trusted instructions — no co-mingling in the same trust context.
evidence: Firewall deployment inventory (all boundaries) + config.
SIEM rule export + sample injection alerts with response.
Architecture proof of trust-context separation.
controls.risk_example: An indirect injection hidden in a supplier PDF instructs the contract-analysis agent to approve payment and forward bank details to an external address.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment injection and adversarial input testing.
Quarterly review of input validation and detection rules.
Review all new retrieval sources, API integrations, email/document pipelines, or tool connections before production onboarding.
Additional testing after injection attempts, security incidents, model changes, prompt changes, or integration changes.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary: 2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
4.3 Fraud, scams, and targeted manipulation
2.1 Compromise of privacy (leaking / inferring sensitive info)
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Malicious natural-language instructions embedded in data (direct or indirect, e.g. a poisoned document) hijack the model to exfiltrate data or take unauthorized actions.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0051 LLM Prompt Injection (Initial Access)
AML.T0051.00…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Prompt injection is the initial-access vector for agents; defence blocks entry analogous to public-app exploitation / phishing.

controls.name: Observability, Anomaly Detection & Incident Response
controls.objective: Emit per-call agent traces, detect behavioural anomalies and run AI-specific incident response.
key_requirements: Every agent runtime emits structured per-call traces (model, prompt, tool calls, decisions, tokens); absence of telemetry is treated as a P1 incident.
Behavioural anomaly detection on agent trajectories feeding the SOC/incident process.
Defined AI-incident runbook with severities and response SLAs.
evidence: Trace schema + coverage report across all runtimes.
Anomaly-detection configuration + sample anomaly cases.
AI-incident runbook + closed-incident records.
controls.risk_example: Slow data exfiltration via repeated inference queries is invisible because the agent emits no call-level traces; it is caught only after the data appears externally.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment validation of logging, monitoring, and alerting.
Continuous or periodic monitoring based on risk tier.
Monthly review for high-risk or operationally impactful systems.
Annual incident response tabletop or playbook review where applicable.
Additional review after incidents, anomalies, major updates, or monitoring failures.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
7.4 Lack of transparency or interpretability
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
2.1 Compromise of privacy (leaking / inferring sensitive info)
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Agent behaviour is opaque; attacks, leakage, or anomalous trajectories go undetected because there is no per-call trace telemetry.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0074 Masquerading (Defense Evasion)
AML.T0024 Exfiltrat…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Trace telemetry + anomaly detection surface impaired defenses, discovery, lateral movement and exfiltration across the killchain.

controls.name: AI Threat Modeling
controls.objective: Model AI-specific and agentic-orchestration threats pre-deployment and map them to mitigations.
key_requirements: Structured AI threat model per system covering model, prompt, memory, agents and integrations — mapped to MITRE ATLAS and OWASP LLM Top 10.
Threat model produced pre-deployment and refreshed on material change.
Each identified threat has an owner and a referenced mitigating control.
evidence: Versioned threat-model artifact (ATLAS/OWASP-tagged).
Threat-to-control traceability matrix.
Refresh history tied to change events.
controls.risk_example: Because no AI threat model was done, the team never considered tool-poisoning of the agent's MCP connectors — the exact path later exploited.
controls.review_cadence:
During design or pre-deployment for applicable systems.
Required before production for systems with external integrations, autonomy, sensitive data, or high-risk workflows.
Review after material architecture, model, data, prompt, tool, connector, vendor, or deployment changes.
Periodic review for high-risk systems.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
7.2 AI possessing dangerous capabilities
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
4.2 Cyberattacks, weapon development or use, and mass harm
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
mappings.mit_risk_closed: AI-specific threats (poisoning, extraction, injection, agent hijacking, excessive agency) are never enumerated because conventional threat models omit the model/prompt/memory/agent surface.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0051 LLM Prompt Injection (Initial Access)
AML.T0020 Po…
mitre_attack: Agentic Orchestration* Autonomous killchain orchestration (no ATT&CK ID yet)
mappings.attack_defense_note: Threat modeling enumerates AI-native and agentic-orchestration attack paths before deployment.

controls.name: AI Security Testing & Red-Teaming
controls.objective: Test AI systems against adversarial, injection, jailbreak and extraction attacks in the release pipeline.
key_requirements: Adversarial test suite (prompt injection, jailbreak, data-leakage extraction, robustness under edge prompts) integrated into the release pipeline.
Defined pass/fail criteria; release blocked on critical findings.
Regression re-test on each material AI change.
evidence: Test-suite definition + latest run results with pass/fail.
Release-gate record (blocked/approved with findings).
Regression history.
controls.risk_example: A model upgrade silently re-opens a jailbreak that was previously closed; without regression security testing it ships to production.
controls.review_cadence:
Pre-deployment security testing.
Regression testing after material changes to models, prompts, data sources, tools, integrations, or system configuration.
Periodic retesting for high-risk or externally exposed systems.
Additional testing after security incidents, detected vulnerabilities, or new threat patterns.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
7.3 Lack of capability or robustness
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.2 AI possessing dangerous capabilities
1.2 Exposure to toxic content
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Model-layer weaknesses (adversarial inputs, jailbreaks, data-leakage extraction, brittleness under edge prompts) ship to production untested.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0043 Craft Adversarial Data
AML.T0054 LLM Jailbreak
AML…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Adversarial testing validates resistance to capability-development misuse and exploitation.

controls.name: Memory & Context Lifecycle Security
controls.objective: Isolate, govern and expire agent memory and context to prevent leakage and poisoned persistence.
key_requirements: Memory isolation enforced between users, tenants and sessions (no cross-bleed).
Context-reuse rules + memory expiry/purge policy defined and enforced.
Poisoned/stale-context detection; memory contents auditable.
evidence: Isolation architecture + test proving no cross-session bleed.
Retention/purge policy + execution logs.
Memory audit/inspection records.
controls.risk_example: User A's data persists in shared agent memory and surfaces in User B's session — a privacy breach created entirely by the memory layer.
controls.review_cadence:
Before deployment for systems using persistent memory or reusable context.
Periodic review based on data sensitivity and risk tier.
Quarterly review for high-risk, regulated, sensitive, or externally exposed systems.
Additional review after changes to memory design, retention rules, access permissions, or incidents involving stored context.

mappings.mit_ai_risk_primary:
2.1 Compromise of privacy (leaking / inferring sensitive info)
2.2 AI system security vulnerabilities and attacks
mappings.mit_ai_risk_secondary:
7.1 AI pursuing its own goals in conflict with human goals/values
3.1 False or misleading information
mappings.mit_risk_closed: Agent/GenAI memory leaks data across users or sessions, retains poisoned context that steers future behaviour, or holds personal data beyond purpose.
mappings.mitre_atlas:
AML.T0057 LLM Data Leakage
AML.T0051.001 LLM Prompt Injectio…
mappings.attack_defense_note: Memory isolation/expiry counter on-host data collection and staging persisted in agent context.
